2603.26632.title: Machine Learning Transferability for Malware Detection
2603.26632.paper_summary: Malware continues to be a predominant operational risk for organizations, especially when obfuscation techniques are used to evade detection. Despite the ongoing efforts in the development of Machine Learning (ML) detection approaches, there is still a lack of feature compatibility in public datasets. This limits generalization when facing distribution shifts, as well as transferability to different datasets. This study evaluates the suitability of different data preprocessing approaches for the detection of Portable Executable (PE) files with ML models. The preprocessing pipeline unifies EMBERv2 (2,381-dim) features datasets, trains paired models under two training setups: EMBER + BODMAS and EMBER + BODMAS + ERMDS. Regarding model evaluation, both EMBER + BODMAS and EMBER + BODMAS + ERMDS models are tested against TRITIUM, INFERNO and SOREL-20M. ERMDS is also used for testing for the EMBER + BODMAS setup.
2603.26632.paper_summary_zh: 惡意軟體仍然是組織面臨的主要操作風險，特別是在使用混淆技術以逃避檢測的情況下。儘管在機器學習（ML）檢測方法的開發上持續努力，但公共數據集中的特徵相容性仍然不足。這限制了在面對分佈變化時的泛化能力，以及對不同數據集的可轉移性。本研究評估了不同數據預處理方法在使用機器學習模型檢測可攜式可執行檔（PE）文件的適用性。預處理流程統一了EMBERv2（2,381維）特徵數據集，並在兩種訓練設置下訓練配對模型：EMBER + BODMAS 和 EMBER + BODMAS + ERMDS。關於模型評估，EMBER + BODMAS 和 EMBER + BODMAS + ERMDS 模型均針對TRITIUM、INFERNO 和 SOREL-20M 進行測試。ERMDS 也用於測試 EMBER + BODMAS 設置。
2603.26632.author: César Vieira et.al.
2603.26632.authors: César Vieira, João Vitorino, Eva Maia, Isabel Praça
